back_translation: Now, the main point of what we are saying is this: we have such a high priest, the One who is enthroned at the right side of the throne of the One of Great Might in the heavens.
He is the servant in the holy place and in the true tabernacle, which the Lord set up, not man.
For every high priest is appointed to offer offerings and sacrifices; therefore it is necessary that this one too should have something to offer.
Now, if He were on the earth, He would not even be a priest at all, because there are already those who offer offerings according to the law.

They serve in a copy-pattern and a shadow of what is in heaven, just as Moses received the warning when he was about to bring the tabernacle to completion. The One spoke: 'Behold, you shall make all things according to the pattern that was shown to you on that mountain.'
But now, in reality, Jesus has received a ministry that is far more excellent — inasmuch as He is the mediator of a better covenant, which has been established on the basis of better divine promises.
For if that first covenant had been faultless, there would not have been any seeking of a place for a second covenant at all.
For when He found fault with them, He spoke: 'Behold, the days are coming, the Lord declares, when I will make a new covenant with the lineage of Israel and with the lineage of Judah,

not according to the covenant that I made with their ancestors, in the day when I grasped their hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they did not continue in My covenant, and I also did not pay attention to them, the Lord declares.

For this is the covenant that I will make with the lineage of Israel after those days, the Lord declares — namely, I will put My laws into their understanding, and I will inscribe those laws on their hearts; and I will be their God, and they will be My people.

And there will no longer be any case where each person teaches his neighbor, or each person teaches his sibling, saying, 'Know the Lord,' because all of them will know Me, from the lowliest of them to the greatest of them.
For I will be merciful toward their unrighteousness, and I will not remember their sins anymore at all.'
When the Lord declared 'new,' He made the first covenant old; and what is becoming old and growing aged is near to vanishing away.
